entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: Staple food crop [Pennisetum glaucum (L.) R Br.] grown in semi-arid tropics of Asia and Africa

entities.description: Region where pearl millet is an important staple food crop

entities.name: CELIAC DISEASE
entities.description: Condition for which pearl millet flour can be used as a substitute for wheat flour

entities.name: TOTAL ANTIOXIDANT CONTENT
entities.description: The overall amount of antioxidants present in pearl millet flour

entities.name: PHENOLIC COMPOUNDS
entities.description: Natural antioxidants present in pearl millet

entities.name: PMIGAP
entities.description: Pearl millet inbred germplasm association panel, a world diversity panel of the crop

entities.name: TOTAL PHENOLIC CONTENT
entities.description: TPC, a measure of phenolic compounds that correlates with antioxidant activity

entities.name: DPPH RADICAL SCAVENGING ACTIVITY
entities.description: A measure of antioxidant activity based on 1,1-diphenyl-2-picrylhydrazyl inhibition

entities.name: FRAP ACTIVITY
entities.description: Ferric-reducing antioxidant power, a measure of antioxidant activity

entities.name: GENOME-WIDE ASSOCIATION STUDIES
entities.description: GWAS, a method used to identify genetic variants associated with specific traits

entities.name: SNPS
entities.description: Single Nucleotide Polymorphisms distributed across pearl millet chromosomes used for association analysis

entities.name: CANDIDATE GENE HARVESTING
entities.description: The process of exploring flanking regions of significantly associated SNPs to identify candidate genes

entities.name: ANTIOXIDANT PATHWAY GENES
entities.description: Genes involved in the biosynthesis of antioxidants

entities.description: A candidate gene related to the antioxidant pathway identified via GWAS

entities.name: GDSL ESTERASE/LIPASE
entities.description: A candidate gene related to the antioxidant pathway identified via GWAS

entities.name: GLUTATHIONE S-TRANSFERASE
entities.description: A candidate gene related to the antioxidant pathway identified via GWAS

entities.name: MOLECULAR BREEDING
entities.description: The use of genetic markers and associated genes to develop high antioxidant-containing cultivars
